message:
fix(session-restore): replace unsafe i64-as-u64/usize casts with
checked decoding

Replace all bare `as u64` and `as usize` casts on SQLite i64 values
with checked decode_u64/decode_usize helpers that return
RestoreError::InvalidPersistedValue on negative or out-of-range values,
preventing silent wrapping on corrupted or adversarial database rows.

Affected functions:
- find_unclean_sessions: created_at, last_checkpoint_at
- load_checkpoint_by_id: checkpoint_at, pane_count, pane_id
- list_sessions: created_at, last_checkpoint_at, checkpoint_count,
  pane_count
- show_session: created_at, last_checkpoint_at, checkpoint_at,
  pane_count, total_bytes
- session_doctor: all 5 aggregate counts

Implementation:
- Add InvalidPersistedValue variant to RestoreError with field name
  and raw i64 value for diagnostics
- Add decode_u64, decode_opt_u64, decode_usize, decode_opt_usize
  helper functions using TryFrom with descriptive error context
- Refactor row mapping to extract raw tuples first, then decode in
  a fallible map step — cleanly separates rusqlite row access from
  value validation

Tests:
- load_latest_checkpoint_rejects_negative_pane_id: inserts pane_id=-1,
  verifies InvalidPersistedValue error
- list_sessions_rejects_negative_created_at: inserts created_at=-1,
  verifies InvalidPersistedValue error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
